title: rode-central: vendor ZIP now contains 'RØDE Central (2.0.111-259).pkg', cask expects 'RØDE Central.pkg'
author: lxhunter
body:
## Description

`brew install --cask rode-central` fails during staging: the cask's `pkg` stanza references `RØDE Central.pkg`, but the vendor ZIP now contains `RØDE Central (2.0.111-259).pkg`.

The download URL (`https://update.rode.com/central/RODE_Central_MACOS.zip`) is unversioned, and RØDE appears to have silently re-packaged the ZIP after the 2.0.111 bump was merged in #258337 (2026-04-09) — the inner pkg filename now carries a version/build suffix.

## Command and error

```
$ brew install --cask rode-central
Error: Could not find PKG source file 'RØDE Central.pkg', found 'RØDE Central (2.0.111-259).pkg' instead.
```

## Evidence

Downloading the vendor ZIP directly and listing its contents:

```
$ curl -fsSL -O https://update.rode.com/central/RODE_Central_MACOS.zip
$ unzip -l RODE_Central_MACOS.zip
...
  RØDE Central (2.0.111-259).pkg
```

The ZIP contains exactly one pkg, named with the `(2.0.111-259)` suffix.

## Environment

```
HOMEBREW_VERSION: 6.0.10
macOS: Tahoe (arm64)
```

`brew doctor`: only unrelated warnings (non-prefixed coreutils in PATH).

title: visual-paradigm: SHA-256 mismatch on 18.0,20260521 (installer re-uploaded upstream)
author: tommiec
body:
`brew install --cask visual-paradigm` fails with a checksum mismatch:

```
Error: Cask reports different checksum: 725c3c81d254d32c7a9f920d23d14a7694be30c52c99d28d09c457f2a24ddd24
SHA-256 checksum of downloaded file: fc3c40f94cf88841170e2fad78f85f3c3465ff6b5f502aa60c23c0262708f19b
```

Reproduced 2026-07-13 on a fresh fetch, so it is not a corrupted cache. It looks like Visual Paradigm replaced the 18.0,20260521 artifact in place.

Since the 18.1 bump (#271475) was closed for failing signature checks, would a checksum-only update of 18.0,20260521 be acceptable in the meantime?

title: codex 0.144.0 cask omits codex-code-mode-host on Apple Silicon
author: galeamarcel
body:
### Verification

  - [ ] This issue's title and/or description do not reference a single formula e.g. `brew install wget`. If they do, open an issue at
  https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-core/issues/new/choose instead.

  ### Provide a detailed description of the proposed feature

  The Homebrew cask `codex` version 0.144.0 on Apple Silicon installs only
  the main `codex` executable:

  `/opt/homebrew/bin/codex`

  Code Mode also requires:

  `/opt/homebrew/bin/codex-code-mode-host`

  Because this executable is not installed, Code Mode fails with:

  `failed to spawn code-mode host /opt/homebrew/bin/codex-code-mode-host: No such file or directory (os error 2)`

  The cask should install the matching `codex-code-mode-host` executable,
  or use the appropriate upstream package containing all required runtime
  executables.

  The official OpenAI release contains the matching asset:

  `codex-code-mode-host-aarch64-apple-darwin.tar.gz`

  https://github.com/openai/codex/releases/tag/rust-v0.144.0

  Environment:

  - macOS on Apple Silicon
  - Homebrew prefix: `/opt/homebrew`
  - Codex version: `0.144.0`

  ### What is the motivation for the feature?

  Code Mode cannot start after installing Codex through the official Homebrew
  cask because a required runtime executable is absent. Reinstalling the same
  cask does not fix the problem because `brew info --cask codex` declares only:

  `codex-aarch64-apple-darwin -> codex`

  ### How will the feature be relevant to at least 90% of Homebrew users?

  This is a packaging correction for the existing `codex` cask rather than a
  general Homebrew feature. For users of this cask who invoke Code Mode, the
  failure is deterministic because the runtime expects an executable that the
  cask does not install.

  If the 90% criterion refers to all Homebrew users, please classify this as a
  cask packaging bug rather than a general feature request.

  ### What alternatives to the feature have been considered?

  - Reinstalling `codex` 0.144.0 does not help because the helper is not declared
    by the cask.
  - Creating a symlink from `codex-code-mode-host` to `codex` is unsafe because
    they are separate upstream executables with different entry points.
  - Installing the helper manually would leave an unmanaged executable in the
    Homebrew prefix and could cause version drift after future upgrades.
  - Switching installation methods would work around the cask packaging problem
    but would not correct it for existing Homebrew users.

title: chatbox: installation fails on case-sensitive APFS due to app bundle name mismatch
author: bcdi
body:

### Description of issue

My system uses a case-sensitive APFS data volume:

```text
File System Personality: Case-sensitive APFS
```

The current cask declares:

```ruby
app "chatbox.app"
```

However, the downloaded DMG contains:

```text
Chatbox.app
```

On the default case-insensitive macOS filesystem this difference is hidden, but on a case-sensitive APFS volume the installation fails because `chatbox.app` and `Chatbox.app` are different paths.

I verified that changing the cask to:

```ruby
app "Chatbox.app"
```

and installing the modified local cask succeeds.

This appears to be a case mismatch between the cask definition and the application bundle contained in the DMG.

title: zed cask upgrade fails to generate shell completions with exit 127
author: htwyay
body:
### Verification

- [x] I understand that [if I ignore these instructions, my issue may be closed without review](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-cask/blob/HEAD/doc/faq/closing_issues_without_review.md).
- [ ] I have retried my command with `--force`.
- [ ] I ran `brew update-reset && brew update` and retried my command.
- [ ] I ran `brew doctor`, fixed as many issues as possible and retried my command.
- [x] I have checked the instructions for [reporting bugs](https://github.com/Homebrew/homebrew-cask#reporting-bugs).
- [x] I made doubly sure this is not a [checksum does not match / SHA256 mismatch](https://docs.brew.sh/Common-Issues#cask---checksum-does-not-match) error (do not open an issue before trying to open a PR to fix first).

### Description of issue

When upgrading the `zed` cask from 1.8.2 to 1.9.0, the app upgrade completed successfully, but Homebrew failed to generate shell completions for bash, zsh, fish, and pwsh.

The failing step appears to run the bare command `zed --completions <shell>` inside Homebrew's cask sandbox. During the upgrade this exited with 127 for all shells.

After installation, the CLI works normally:

```
$ zed --version
Zed 1.9.0 – /Applications/Zed.app
```

Manual completion generation also works outside the cask sandbox.

However, the generated completion files were not installed:
```
ls: /opt/homebrew/etc/bash_completion.d/zed: No such file or directory
ls: /opt/homebrew/share/fish/vendor_completions.d/zed.fish: No such file or directory
ls: /opt/homebrew/share/pwsh/completions/_zed.ps1: No such file or directory
ls: /opt/homebrew/share/zsh/site-functions/_zed: No such file or directory
```

This looks related to the cask stanza:
```rb
generate_completions_from_executable "zed", "--completions",
  shells: [:bash, :zsh, :fish, :pwsh]
```
Possibly the sandboxed completion-generation environment cannot resolve the newly linked `opt/homebrew/bin/zed` during the cask install phase.

### Command that failed

brew upgrade -v

### Output of command with `--verbose --debug`

```
I did not capture this with --verbose --debug at the time. Original output:
```

### Description of issue


## Insta360 Studio Quick Look plugin is installed but not registered when installed via Homebrew Cask

### Description

When installing Insta360 Studio using Homebrew Cask:

```bash
brew install --cask insta360-studio
````

the Quick Look plugin appears to be included inside the application bundle, but it is not registered with macOS. As a result, Finder previews for Insta360 files (e.g. `.insp`) do not work.

The official Insta360 installer seems to perform an additional registration step, which is missing when installing via Homebrew.

### Steps to reproduce

1. Install Insta360 Studio via Homebrew:

```bash
brew install --cask insta360-studio
```

2. Open Finder and select an Insta360 file (`.insp`).

3. Press Space to show Quick Look preview.

### Expected behavior

The Quick Look extension should be available immediately after installation.

### Actual behavior

No Quick Look preview is available.

The extension is present in the app bundle:

```
/Applications/Insta360 Studio.app/Contents/Plugins/QuickLookHost.app
```

but it is not registered.

### Workaround

Manually launching the Quick Look host registers the extension:

```bash
open "/Applications/Insta360 Studio.app/Contents/Plugins/QuickLookHost.app"
```

After running this once, Quick Look works correctly.

The registration can be verified with:

```bash
pluginkit -m | grep -i QuickLookHost
```

### Possible fix

Could the cask add a `postflight` step to launch/register the Quick Look extension after installation, or otherwise provide a hint to the user that this additional step is required?

Thank you!

As written above an installation or upgrade of eigent to version 1.0.0, fails with a sha mismatch.

After searching, I've found a solution in [#3778](https://github.com/orgs/Homebrew/discussions/3778#discussion-4470951)
